title: Scaffold 3D graph component with react-force-graph-3d
description:
Install react-force-graph-3d and wire up a minimal 3D graph that renders categories and posts as basic nodes with edges. This is the foundation all other children build on.

## Scope
1. Install `react-force-graph-3d` (and `three` peer dep)
2. Create `components/productivity/productivity-graph-3d.tsx` as a new "use client" component
3. Accept the same props as the current ProductivityGraph (categories, posts, allPosts, draftSlugs)
4. Build graph data (nodes + links) from props — reuse existing buildGraph logic where possible
5. Render a ForceGraph3D instance with:
   - Basic sphere nodes (categories) and box nodes (posts)
   - Simple line edges connecting posts to categories
   - d3-force-3d layout with categories constrained to upper Z-plane and posts to lower Z-plane
6. Wire up the page component (app/productivity/page.tsx) to conditionally render the 3D graph (feature-flagged or branch-only)
7. Remove sigma.js / graphology / ForceAtlas2 deps from this branch

## Acceptance criteria
1. 3D graph renders in the browser with orbit camera
2. Categories appear on a higher Z-plane than posts
3. Edges connect posts to their categories
4. No sigma.js code remains on this branch
5. Component accepts identical props to the 2D version
close_reason: PR #2 merged after PR review

title: Fix broken assets on GitHub Pages deploy (basePath mismatch)
description:
When navigating to tze.how/site-v2, only text/hyperlinks render — all styling and interactivity is broken. The browser console shows 404s for all JS chunks and font preload warnings.

**Symptoms:**
- All `_next/static/chunks/*.js` files return 404
- Font files preloaded but never used (JS not loading so React never hydrates)
- Site is at `/site-v2` but assets are requested from `/_next/` (root) instead of `/site-v2/_next/`

**Root cause (likely):**
Next.js `basePath` is not set (or not set correctly) in next.config. When deployed to GitHub Pages under a subpath (`/site-v2`), all asset URLs need to be prefixed with `/site-v2`. Without `basePath: '/site-v2'`, Next.js generates asset paths relative to the domain root.

**Console errors:**
```
GET https://tze.how/_next/static/chunks/turbopack-d7cc979e65870564.js 404
GET https://tze.how/_next/static/chunks/aee6c7720838f8a2.js 404
GET https://tze.how/_next/static/chunks/3e089b3194338431.js 404
(+ more JS chunks and font preload warnings)
```

**Fix should include:**
1. Set `basePath: '/site-v2'` in next.config (or whatever the correct subpath is)
2. Possibly set `assetPrefix` as well
3. Verify the GitHub Actions workflow / static export config aligns
4. Test that all assets load correctly after deploy
close_reason: Added basePath: /site-v2 to next.config.ts so all assets resolve under the correct subpath

title: Reconcile spec-to-code coverage for 3D graph migration
description:
Deep-dive review: compare the original spec/requirements (see epic description) against the implementation delivered by sibling beads under this epic.

Workflow:
1. Re-read the epic description and all sibling bead descriptions/acceptance criteria.
2. Audit the codebase changes delivered by each sibling bead.
3. Produce a checklist mapping every spec requirement to its implementing bead.
4. For any requirement NOT covered (or only partially covered):
   a. Create a new child bead under this epic describing the missing work.
   b. Set appropriate priority and link dependencies.
   c. Do NOT create another reconciliation bead.
5. Keep this reconciliation bead open (or blocked) until all newly created gap beads are closed.
6. Re-run the requirement-to-bead checklist and close this bead only when all requirements show full coverage.
notes:
Reconciliation found follow-up gaps; blocked pending site-v2-znn and site-v2-jcc.
All gap beads closed; rerunning reconciliation for final closure.
close_reason: Reconciliation rerun completed with full coverage

title: Rewrite tze.how as Editorial Minimal publication site
description:
Full rewrite of tze.how — a personal site as a publication, not a dashboard.

## Theme: Editorial Minimal

Quiet, confident, literate. Slightly academic but modern.
Think: a personal column in a serious magazine written by an engineer.
The design disappears so the content feels deliberate and trustworthy.

## Tech Stack

- **Next.js 16** (App Router, server components, static generation)
- **React 19** with TypeScript strict mode
- **Tailwind CSS 4.1** (v4 CSS-first config)
- **Bun** as package manager and runtime
- **MDX / rendered markdown** for blog (file-based, no CMS)
- **shiki / rehype-pretty-code** for syntax highlighting
- **next-mdx-remote** or similar for markdown processing

No animation libraries (no Framer Motion, no GSAP, no R3F). Typography and whitespace carry the design.

## Pages

- **Profile** (`/`) — One-liner hero statement, contact links, bio as prose, projects as annotated bibliography entries
- **Blog** (`/blog`) — Table-of-contents index, long-form essay posts rendered from markdown
- **Resume** (`/resume`) — Embedded PDF or styled HTML with download

Navigation is a publication masthead: `TH — Software Engineer · Profile · Blog · Resume · ☾`

## Visual Language

- **Layout**: Vertically structured, centered. Narrow readable column (~65–75ch). Large intentional margins. No sidebars, no card grids, no multi-column.
- **Typography**: The main design feature. Book-like headings, comfortable line spacing (1.6–1.8), hierarchy via size not weight. Serif or quality sans-serif (e.g. IBM Plex, Literata, Inter).
- **Color**: Rare and intentional. Light mode = paper white + dark ink + calm blue links. Dark mode = deep ink + soft off-white (feels like reading at night, not a code editor).
- **Interaction**: Very restrained. Hover underlines, focus outlines, dark/light toggle only. No animations, no parallax, no 3D, no hover transforms.

## Projects (displayed as bibliography entries, not cards)

1. OpenAI Car Racing — imitation learning, LRP visualization
2. Automated Barista — Niryo One robotic arm via ROS
3. Ethereum Activity Analyzer — MAS compliance analysis
4. i3 Ricing — tiling WM customization
5. Handwaving Magicka — CV-based gesture game controller
6. Badminton Court Bot — Telegram availability checker
7. SUTD-MIT Global Leadership — electric boat
8. Binary Sudoku — FPGA with programmable LEDs
9. Myx: Food Ordering — Vue-based delivery platform

## Source

Design spec: README.md in this repo
close_reason: All scoped implementation tasks completed and validated

title: Add Spotlight/command palette search feature

As the user types, matching pages (blog posts, projects, resume, about) appear in a dropdown list. User can navigate with arrow keys and Enter to select, which navigates to that page. The overlay should dismiss on Escape or clicking outside. Should search across page titles, blog post summaries/tags, and project names. Keep it minimal and consistent with the editorial design - no heavy animations, just a clean modal with results.

title: Refactor blog content to content-bundle structure
description:
Migrate from flat content/blog/*.mdx + public/images/blog/{slug}-{N}.{ext} to co-located content bundles:

content/blog/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}/
  index.mdx
  thumbnail.{ext}
  {other assets}

Changes required:
1. Create new directory structure - one folder per post named {YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}/
2. Move each .mdx file into its folder as index.mdx
3. Move corresponding images from public/images/blog/{slug}-*.{ext} into each post folder
4. Rename {slug}-0.{ext} to thumbnail.{ext}, rename remaining {slug}-{N}.{ext} to descriptive or sequential names
5. Update lib/blog.ts:
   - getPostSlugs(): scan for directories containing index.mdx instead of flat .mdx files
   - readPostFile(): read {dir}/index.mdx instead of {slug}.mdx
   - slugFromFileName(): derive slug by stripping YYYY-MM-DD- prefix from directory name
   - getPostThumbnail(): look for thumbnail.{ext} inside the post directory instead of public/images/blog
6. Update image references in MDX files from /images/blog/{slug}-{N}.{ext} to relative paths ./filename.{ext}
7. Configure Next.js to serve content/blog assets (or copy them to public at build time)
8. Update CLAUDE.md architecture section to reflect new structure
9. Remove public/images/blog/ after migration (assets now co-located)
10. Verify: bun build, bun lint, spot-check blog pages
close_reason: Migrated blog from flat content/blog/*.mdx + public/images/blog/ to co-located content bundles at content/blog/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}/index.mdx with thumbnail.{ext} and assets. Updated lib/blog.ts, lib/projects.ts, generate-feed.mjs, MDX image paths. Added copy-blog-assets.mjs prebuild script and .gitignore entry for generated public/blog/. Updated CLAUDE.md. All quality gates pass.

title: Implement custom node rendering (shapes, thumbnails, borders)
description:
Replace default node geometries with custom Three.js objects that match the current visual design: circles for categories, squares for blogposts, thumbnail textures, and border-only fallback.

## Scope
1. Categories: flat circle geometry (or disc sprite) with HSL-generated colors (reuse golden-angle hue distribution)
2. Published posts with thumbnail: square plane with the thumbnail image loaded as a Three.js texture
3. Published posts without thumbnail: square wireframe (border-only) in POST_COLOR (#3b82f6)
4. Draft posts: same as above but in POST_DRAFT_COLOR (#94a3b8) with dimmed edges
5. Node sizing: categories at CATEGORY_SIZE, posts at POST_BASE_SIZE + normalizeSize * POST_SIZE_STEP
6. Use `nodeThreeObject` callback on ForceGraph3D to return custom meshes/sprites

## Acceptance criteria
1. Categories render as colored circles/discs
2. Posts with thumbnails render as square planes with the image texture
3. Posts without thumbnails render as square wireframes (hollow squares)
4. Draft posts use muted gray color
5. Node sizes match relative proportions of current 2D graph
close_reason: PR #3 merged after PR review

title: Match semantic highlight parity by brightening connected visible nodes
description: Gap from reconciliation site-v2-8xh: semantic filtering currently brightens only the active pinned node. Update interaction visual-state logic so connected visible nodes are brightened per spec while unrelated nodes remain dimmed.
close_reason: Simple change merged to main

title: Constrain camera and polish 3D presentation
description:
Lock down camera behavior so the 3D graph feels intentional, not like a free-fly sandbox. Polish the visual presentation.

## Scope

### Camera
1. Initial camera position: slight tilt from above (e.g. 30-40° elevation) looking at center
2. Constrain orbit: allow horizontal rotation + limited vertical tilt, prevent flipping below the graph plane
3. Zoom limits: min/max distance matching current minCameraRatio/maxCameraRatio feel
4. Animated reset: smooth transition back to initial camera on "Reset view"
5. Disable auto-rotation (if default in react-force-graph-3d)

### Edges
1. Curved edges between posts and categories (matching current curvature variation per edge)
2. Edge color matches category color (dimmed for drafts)
3. Edges render behind/between the two Z-planes

### Polish
1. Anti-aliasing enabled
2. Container matches current sizing: 360px mobile, 560px desktop, full breakout width
3. Loading indicator during initial force layout
4. Smooth force layout: settle quickly then freeze (warmupTicks / cooldownTicks tuning)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Camera starts tilted showing depth between planes
2. Orbit is constrained (no flipping under the graph)
3. Animated camera reset works
4. Edges are curved and colored per category
5. Force layout settles in <2 seconds and freezes
close_reason: PR #6 merged after PR review

title: Rewrite /workflows as /setup with interactive category graph
description:
Rewrite the current /workflows page into a new /setup page featuring an interactive graph visualization of categorized nodes.

## Current State
- `/workflows` route exists at `app/workflows/page.tsx` with hardcoded workflow groups linking to blog posts
- 14 blog posts exist in `content/blog/` with YAML frontmatter (title, date, summary, tags)

## Requirements

### Route Rename
- Rename `/workflows` → `/setup`
- Update all navigation links (masthead, footer, etc.) that reference `/workflows`
- Delete `app/workflows/page.tsx`, create `app/setup/page.tsx`

### Graph Visualization
- Render an interactive graph with two node types:
  1. **Category nodes** — hardcoded set representing personal setup domains
  2. **Blogpost nodes** — individual blog posts that tag one or more categories

### Categories (hardcoded)
The following categories form the fixed set of category nodes:
- hardware
- software
- homelab
- smart home
- terminal
- IDE
- programming
- agents
- Linux
- Windows
- learnings

### Blog Post → Category Linking
- Blog posts declare which categories they belong to via their frontmatter tags
- A blog post node connects to every category node whose tag it includes
- Only posts with the `#active` tag in their frontmatter are rendered in the graph (allows "turning off" old/outdated setups)

### Node Sizing via `#size-N` Annotation
- Blog posts can include a `#size-1` through `#size-5` tag in frontmatter to control visual weight/size of their node in the graph
- Default size if no annotation: `size-2`
- Larger numbers = bigger nodes

### Example Frontmatter
```yaml
---
title: "Setting Up i3wm Ricing"
date: 2026-01-15
summary: "My i3wm window manager configuration..."
tags: ["Linux", "terminal", "software", "active", "size-3"]
---
```
This post would:
- Appear in the graph (has `active` tag)
- Connect to Linux, terminal, and software category nodes
- Render at size 3

### Design Constraints
- Must follow the "quiet, confident, literate" design philosophy
- No flashy animations or heavy decoration
- Graph should be readable and functional, not a tech demo
- Use the project color palette (light: #fafaf9 bg, #1c1917 text; dark: #171412 bg, #e7e5e4 text)
- Consider a library like d3-force, react-flow, or similar for graph layout

### Technical Considerations
- Static site export compatibility (no server-side rendering at runtime)
- Graph must be a client component (interactive)
- Blog data loading should reuse existing `lib/blog.ts` infrastructure
- Category definitions should live in a shared config (e.g., `lib/setup-categories.ts`)
notes:
## Additional Requirements (added later)

### Blogpost Node Icons
- Blogpost nodes in the graph should display the associated blog post image as the node icon/thumbnail
- Images follow the existing convention: `public/` or content-adjacent images named like `setting-up-of-i3wm-ricing-0.jpg`
- If no image exists for a post, fall back to a text-only node or generic icon

### Graph Library Decision
- Use `d3-force` standalone (~15KB) for force-directed layout, NOT the full d3 library
- Render with React + SVG — node interaction (hover, click, drag) via React event handlers
- Alternative: deterministic radial layout with zero deps if d3-force proves unnecessary
close_reason: Replaced workflows with interactive productivity graph route and updated navigation/search/sitemap

title: Add SRE category and "Homelab Observability" placeholder blogpost

title: Support #draft tag to hide posts from listings
description:
Add a `draft` tag convention for blog posts. Posts tagged `draft` should be excluded from all listing surfaces but remain accessible via direct URL.

## Behavior
- Posts with `draft` in their tags array are **hidden** from:
  - `/blog` index listing
  - `/setup` graph (even if they also have `active`)
  - Any other aggregated post lists (related posts, etc.)
- Posts with `draft` are **still accessible** via direct link (`/blog/[slug]`)
- `draft` takes precedence over `active` — a post tagged both `draft` and `active` is hidden from listings

## Implementation
- Filter out draft posts in `getAllPostSummaries()` or add a separate helper (e.g., `getPublishedPostSummaries()`) so all listing surfaces use it
- The `/blog/[slug]` static params generation should still include drafts so direct links work
- Consider a `isDraft(post)` utility for clarity

## Immediate Use
- Tag all placeholder blog posts (e.g., the upcoming `homelab-observability.mdx` from site-v2-lds.1) with `draft`
close_reason: Implemented draft-tag filtering for listing surfaces while preserving direct slug access

title: Migrate blog posts from ButterCMS to local MDX
description: Fetch all blog posts from the existing ButterCMS instance and convert them to local MDX files in content/blog/. Each post should preserve: title, slug, published date, summary/meta description, body content (converted from HTML to MDX), tags/categories, and any images (downloaded to public/images/blog/). The ButterCMS API docs are at https://buttercms.com/docs/api/. Use the Posts endpoint to list and retrieve all posts.
close_reason: Migrated 6 posts from ButterCMS with 38 images downloaded to local storage

title: Implement interactions (hover, click, pin, filter, fullscreen)
description:
Port all existing interaction behaviors from the 2D graph to the 3D version.

## Scope

### Hover
- Post nodes: show info panel (thumbnail, title, summary, category tags) at top-left of container
- Category nodes: set focused state (highlight connected nodes)
- Cursor changes: pointer on hover, grab on background

### Click
- Post nodes: navigate to /blog/{slug}
- Category nodes: toggle pin/unpin (persistent focus)
- Background: clear pin and focused state

### Semantic filtering (pin/focus)
- Pinned category: brighten category + connected posts + their other categories; dim everything else (18% opacity, 70% size)
- Pinned post: brighten post + connected categories; dim everything else
- Edges: brighten connected edges, dim others (12% opacity)
- Active/pinned nodes: 30% larger, brightened color

### Fullscreen
- Toggle via button
- Escape to exit
- Container goes h-screen w-screen, hides overflow on body

### Zoom controls
- Zoom in/out buttons
- Reset view button (resets camera + unpins)
- Rebalance button (re-runs force layout)

### Keyboard
- Escape: exit fullscreen

## Acceptance criteria
1. Clicking a post navigates to its blog page
2. Clicking a category pins focus to it and dims unrelated nodes
3. Clicking background unpins
4. Hover shows info panel for posts (with thumbnail, title, summary)
5. Fullscreen toggle works with Escape to exit
6. Zoom in/out/reset buttons functional
7. Rebalance button re-runs layout
close_reason: PR #5 merged after PR review

title: Auto-infer tags for existing blog posts
description: Use LLM to read each existing blog post and auto-infer relevant tags/categories, then add them to the post's frontmatter. This ensures all posts have tags before the category filtering feature is used. Should: 1) Read all existing blog posts in content/blog/, 2) Analyze content to infer appropriate tags, 3) Add tags array to each post's YAML frontmatter. This task should be completed before the filtering UI is built.
close_reason: Inferred tags for all blog posts and updated frontmatter.

title: Add 3D label rendering (bold categories, italic transparent posts)
description:
Implement text labels for graph nodes using Three.js text sprites or CSS2DRenderer, matching the current label styles.

## Scope
1. Category labels: bold weight, full opacity, rendered with the resolved Inter font
2. Post labels: italic style, 50% opacity, bracketed format ("[Post Title]")
3. Labels should billboard (always face camera)
4. Labels appear near nodes (offset to the right or below, avoiding overlap with node geometry)
5. Label visibility: only show labels above a zoom threshold (equivalent to current POST_LABEL_ZOOM_THRESHOLD = 1.35)
6. Label density: avoid cluttering by hiding labels when too many overlap (sigma's labelDensity equivalent)

## Design considerations
- CSS2DRenderer (HTML overlay) gives better text quality but may conflict with Three.js raycasting
- SpriteText (canvas-to-texture) integrates with 3D but looks fuzzy at small sizes
- Evaluate both; CSS2DRenderer is recommended for text crispness

## Acceptance criteria
1. Category labels render bold, full opacity
2. Post labels render italic, 50% transparent
3. Labels face the camera at all angles
4. Labels hide when zoomed out beyond threshold
5. Font matches the Inter font loaded by the site
close_reason: PR #4 merged after PR review

title: Add reading time to blog posts
description: Show estimated reading time (e.g. '5 min read') next to the date on blog post pages and in the blog index. Calculate from word count at build time.
close_reason: Rendered reading time on blog index and post metadata

title: Add theme-aware colors and dark mode switching
description:
Make the 3D graph respond to light/dark theme changes, matching the current behavior.

## Scope
1. Read theme palette from CSS variables (--background, --foreground, --muted, --rule)
2. MutationObserver on document.documentElement for data-theme/style attribute changes
3. On theme change: update scene background, node colors, edge colors, label colors
4. Category colors: golden-angle HSL distribution with theme-aware saturation/lightness (dark: s=44 l=66, light: s=40 l=74)
5. Edge colors: category color for published, dimmed for drafts
6. Scene background: match --background CSS variable
7. Post colors: #3b82f6 published, #94a3b8 draft (same in both themes)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Toggling theme updates all graph colors without re-mount
2. Scene background matches page background in both themes
3. Category colors shift saturation/lightness per theme
4. No flash or re-layout on theme switch
close_reason: Simple change merged to main

title: Replace 2D sigma.js graph with 3D Three.js visualization
description:
Replace the productivity graph's 2D sigma.js renderer with a true 3D visualization using react-force-graph-3d (Three.js). The graph should render as two distinct Z-planes: categories floating above blogposts, viewed from a slight tilt angle.

## Current state
- `components/productivity/productivity-graph.tsx` (1073 lines)
- Stack: sigma.js v3 + graphology + ForceAtlas2 + @sigma/node-image + @sigma/edge-curve
- Features: hover tooltips, click-to-navigate posts, pin/focus categories, semantic filtering (dim unrelated nodes), fullscreen, zoom/pan, theme-aware (light/dark), draft preview mode, custom label rendering, curved edges

## Target state
- Stack: react-force-graph-3d (wraps Three.js + d3-force-3d)
- Two Z-planes: categories on upper plane, blogposts on lower plane
- Slight camera tilt to show depth between planes
- Node shapes: circles for categories, squares for blogposts
- Blogpost thumbnails rendered as textured square sprites
- Border-only squares for posts without thumbnails
- Labels: bold for categories, italic semi-transparent for posts
- All existing interactions preserved (hover, click, pin, filter, fullscreen, zoom)
- Theme-aware colors (light/dark switching via MutationObserver)
- Draft preview mode via ?drafts=true

## What stays the same
- Page component (app/productivity/page.tsx) and data model
- ProductivityGraphCategory / ProductivityGraphPost types
- Category color generation (golden-angle HSL distribution)
- All utility functions (dimColor, brightenColor, hexToRgb, etc.)

## Non-goals
- No physics-based animation (settle-then-freeze like current ForceAtlas2)
- No orbit controls (constrained camera, not free-fly)
- No per-node 3D geometry (no extruded shapes, just sprites/planes)
close_reason: All dependent migration and reconciliation beads completed and merged

title: Build root layout with masthead navigation, footer, and dark/light theme system

Implement dark/light toggle with localStorage persistence. Set up typography system (IBM Plex or similar), color tokens per spec (light: #fafaf9/#1c1917, dark: #171412/#e7e5e4), and base Tailwind theme. Content column capped at ~65-75ch with generous margins.
close_reason: Implemented root layout, masthead, footer, and theme toggle with tokens
